International football competition

The **2024 Copa CONMEBOL Sudamericana** was the 23rd edition of the [CONMEBOL Sudamericana](/wiki/Copa_Sudamericana "Copa Sudamericana") (also referred to as the *Copa Sudamericana*), [South America](/wiki/South_America "South America")'s secondary club [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") tournament organized by [CONMEBOL](/wiki/CONMEBOL "CONMEBOL"). The competition began on 5 March and ended on 23 November 2024, with the [final](/wiki/2024_Copa_Sudamericana_final "2024 Copa Sudamericana final") played in [Asunción](/wiki/Asunci%C3%B3n "Asunción"), Paraguay.[[1]](#cite_note-1) [Estadio General Pablo Rojas](/wiki/Estadio_General_Pablo_Rojas "Estadio General Pablo Rojas"), also known as "La Nueva Olla", was announced as the host stadium for the final match on 7 October 2024.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Argentine club [Racing](/wiki/Racing_Club_de_Avellaneda "Racing Club de Avellaneda") were the champions, winning their first title in the competition after defeating Brazilian side [Cruzeiro](/wiki/Cruzeiro_Esporte_Clube "Cruzeiro Esporte Clube") 3–1 in the final.[[3]](#cite_note-3) As winners of the 2024 Copa Sudamericana, Racing earned the right to play against the winners of the [2024 Copa Libertadores](/wiki/2024_Copa_Libertadores "2024 Copa Libertadores") in the [2025 Recopa Sudamericana](/wiki/2025_Recopa_Sudamericana "2025 Recopa Sudamericana") and also automatically qualified for the [2025 Copa Libertadores](/wiki/2025_Copa_Libertadores "2025 Copa Libertadores") group stage.

[LDU Quito](/wiki/L.D.U._Quito "L.D.U. Quito") were the titleholders, defending their title after placing third in their Copa Libertadores group, but they were eliminated by [Lanús](/wiki/Club_Atl%C3%A9tico_Lan%C3%BAs "Club Atlético Lanús") in the round of 16.

## Teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2024_Copa_Sudamericana&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Teams")]

The following 44 teams from the 10 CONMEBOL associations qualified for the tournament:[[4]](#cite_note-4)

* Argentina and Brazil: 6 berths each
* All other associations: 4 berths each

The entry stage is determined as follows:[[5]](#cite_note-changes2021-5)[[6]](#cite_note-regulations-6)

* Group stage: 12 teams (teams from Argentina and Brazil)
* First stage: 32 teams (teams from all other associations)

## Schedule

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2024_Copa_Sudamericana&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Schedule")]

The schedule of the competition was as follows:[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)

| Stage | Draw date | First leg | Second leg |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First stage | 19 December 2023[[19]](#cite_note-19) | 5–7 March 2024 | |
| Group stage | 18 March 2024[[20]](#cite_note-20) | * Matchday 1: 2–4 April 2024 * Matchday 2: 9–11 April 2024 * Matchday 3: 23–25 April 2024 * Matchday 4: 7–9 May 2024 * Matchday 5: 14–16 May 2024 * Matchday 6: 28–30 May 2024 | |
| Knockout round play-offs | *No draw* | 16–18 July 2024 | 23–25 July 2024 |
| Round of 16 | 3 June 2024 | 13–15 August 2024 | 20–22 August 2024 |
| Quarter-finals | 17–19 September 2024 | 24–26 September 2024 |
| Semi-finals | 23–24 October 2024 | 30–31 October 2024 |
| Final | 23 November 2024 at [Estadio General Pablo Rojas](/wiki/Estadio_General_Pablo_Rojas "Estadio General Pablo Rojas"), [Asunción](/wiki/Asunci%C3%B3n "Asunción") | |

The draw for the first stage was held on 19 December 2023, 12:00 [PYST](/wiki/Time_in_Paraguay "Time in Paraguay") ([UTC−3](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9203:00 "UTC−03:00")), at the CONMEBOL Convention Centre in [Luque](/wiki/Luque "Luque"), [Paraguay](/wiki/Paraguay "Paraguay").[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)

For the first stage, the 32 teams involved were divided into eight pots according to their national association. The 32 teams were drawn into 16 ties, with the four teams from each national association being drawn against a rival from the same association in two ties per association.[[23]](#cite_note-draw_procedure-23)

The draw for the group stage was held on 18 March 2024, 20:00 [PYST](/wiki/Time_in_Paraguay "Time in Paraguay") ([UTC−3](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9203:00 "UTC−03:00")), at the CONMEBOL Convention Centre in [Luque](/wiki/Luque "Luque"), [Paraguay](/wiki/Paraguay "Paraguay").[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25)

Teams were seeded by their *CONMEBOL Clubs ranking* as of 18 December 2023 (shown in parentheses),[[26]](#cite_note-ranking-26) taking into account the following three factors:[[27]](#cite_note-27)

1. Performance in the last 10 years, taking into account Copa Libertadores and Copa Sudamericana results in the period 2014–2023.
2. Historical coefficient, taking into account Copa Libertadores and Copa Sudamericana results in the period 1960–2013 and 2002–2013 respectively.
3. Local tournament champion, with bonus points awarded to domestic league champions of the last 10 years.

For the group stage, the 32 teams were drawn into eight groups (Groups A–H) of four containing a team from each of the four pots. The four losers of the [Copa Libertadores third stage](/wiki/2024_Copa_Libertadores_qualifying_stages#Third_stage "2024 Copa Libertadores qualifying stages") were seeded into Pot 4. Teams from the same association could not be drawn into the same group.

## Group stage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2024_Copa_Sudamericana&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Group stage")]

Main article: [2024 Copa Sudamericana group stage](/wiki/2024_Copa_Sudamericana_group_stage "2024 Copa Sudamericana group stage")

In the group stage, each group was played on a home-and-away [round-robin](/wiki/Round-robin_tournament "Round-robin tournament") basis. The teams were ranked according to the following criteria: 1. [Points](/wiki/Points_(association_football) "Points (association football)") (3 points for a win, 1 point for a draw, and 0 points for a loss); 2. [Goal difference](/wiki/Goal_difference "Goal difference"); 3. Goals scored; 4. Away goals scored; 5. CONMEBOL ranking (Regulations Article 2.4.2).[[6]](#cite_note-regulations-6)

The winners of each group advanced to the [round of 16](/wiki/2024_Copa_Sudamericana_final_stages#Round_of_16 "2024 Copa Sudamericana final stages") of the [final stages](/wiki/2024_Copa_Sudamericana_final_stages "2024 Copa Sudamericana final stages"), whilst the runners-up of each group advanced to the [knockout round play-offs](/wiki/2024_Copa_Sudamericana_final_stages#Knockout_round_play-offs "2024 Copa Sudamericana final stages") of the final stages.

## Final stages

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2024_Copa_Sudamericana&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Final stages")]

Main article: [2024 Copa Sudamericana final stages](/wiki/2024_Copa_Sudamericana_final_stages "2024 Copa Sudamericana final stages")

Starting from the knockout round play-offs, the teams played a [single-elimination tournament](/wiki/Single-elimination_tournament "Single-elimination tournament") with the following rules:[[6]](#cite_note-regulations-6)

* In the knockout round play-offs, round of 16, quarter-finals and semi-finals, each tie was played on a home-and-away [two-legged](/wiki/Two-legged_tie "Two-legged tie") basis, with the higher-seeded team hosting the second leg (Regulations Article 2.2.3). If tied on aggregate, [extra time](/wiki/Overtime_(sports)#Association_football "Overtime (sports)") would not be played, and a [penalty shoot-out](/wiki/Penalty_shoot-out_(association_football) "Penalty shoot-out (association football)") was used to determine the winners (Regulations Article 2.4.4).
* The final was played as a single match at a venue pre-selected by CONMEBOL, with the higher-seeded team designated as the "home" team for administrative purposes (Regulations Article 2.2.6). If tied after regulation, 30 minutes of [extra time](/wiki/Overtime_(sports)#Association_football "Overtime (sports)") would be played. If still tied after extra time, a [penalty shoot-out](/wiki/Penalty_shoot-out_(association_football) "Penalty shoot-out (association football)") would be used to determine the winners (Regulations Article 2.4.5).

### Seeding

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2024_Copa_Sudamericana&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Seeding")]

For the final stages, the teams were seeded according to their results in the group stage, with the Copa Sudamericana group winners seeded 1–8, the Copa Sudamericana group runners-up seeded 9–16, and the Copa Libertadores group third-placed teams seeded 17–24. For the round of 16 draw, the seeds 1–8 made up Pot 1, and the eight knockout round play-offs winners (seeds 9–24) made up Pot 2, keeping their seed. Teams from the same association could play each other from the knockout round play-offs onwards.
